_domain_hint: Iran / Israel / Houthi / IRGC theatre. Sources: Reuters, AP, Al Jazeera, BBC, IDF spokesperson, IRGC statements, Iranian state media (IRNA/Tasnim/Mehr/Press TV), UKMTO maritime bulletins, ISW Iran updates.
claim: The United States and Iran reached a definitive long-term peace agreement on April 8, 2026.
claim_origin: Misreading of Trump Truth Social statement language

sources.text: We are very far along with a definitive Agreement concerning Longterm PEACE with Iran, and PEACE in the Middle East. We received a 10 point proposal from Iran, and believe it is a workable basis on which to negotiate. Almost all of the various points of past contention have been agreed to between the United States and Iran, but a two week period will allow the Agreement to be finalized and consummated. On behalf of the United States of America, as President, and also representing the Countries of the Middle East, it is an Honor to have this Longterm problem close to resolution.

I invite their delegations to Islamabad on Friday, 10th April 2026, to further negotiate for a conclusive agreement to settle all disputes. We earnestly hope, that the 'Islamabad Talks' succeed in achieving sustainable peace and wish to share more good news in coming days.

If attacks against Iran are halted, our Powerful Armed Forces will cease their defensive operations. For a period of two weeks, safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz will be possible via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces.

gold.rationale_summary: The April 8 announcements describe a 2-week ceasefire and a 10-point Iranian proposal as a 'workable basis on which to negotiate' — explicitly preliminary. Trump states 'a two week period will allow the Agreement to be finalized and consummated' — i.e., the agreement is NOT yet finalized. Sharif invites delegations to Islamabad April 10 to 'further negotiate for a conclusive agreement' — clearly framing this as future-looking. A ceasefire is not a peace agreement; an agreement-to-negotiate is not a definitive deal.
required_caveats: A 2-week ceasefire was confirmed, but a definitive long-term peace agreement was NOT reached on April 8.
Trump's language ('close to resolution', 'workable basis', 'will be finalized') is aspirational and forward-looking, not declarative of a completed agreement.
